summary: Frontend-only Playwright testing of clouDroplets Awwwards redesign at desktop (1440x900) and mobile (390x844). Covered hero, sticky nav + smooth scroll, mobile menu, services accordion, stats counters, partners marquee, contact form (empty + valid submit), toasts, form clear, footer. 18/20 checks passed. One real bug found: the first stat counter '250+' (Projects Complete) is stuck at 0+ while the other 3 count up correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: First stats counter renders '0+' instead of counting up to 250+. Other three counters (120+, 16, 40+) animate correctly. Reproduced twice by scrolling section into view center and waiting 3.5s. Likely a stale closure / StrictMode double-invoke / useInView 'once' interaction that only affects the first Counter instance.

action_items: Fix Stats.jsx first counter (value=250) not animating to final number. Consider (a) removing the cleanup cancelAnimationFrame race with StrictMode, (b) using a ref to hold the raf id, and (c) always setting setN(value) once inView becomes true as a safety fallback.
critical_code_review_comments: Stats.jsx: In useEffect cleanup, cancelAnimationFrame(raf) reads the outer 'raf' variable which is reassigned inside tick(). Under React StrictMode dev double-invocation this can cancel a freshly-scheduled RAF from the second run. Recommend storing raf id in a useRef and doing an explicit setN(value) at animation end.

Contact.jsx: form only shows a local sonner toast and does not persist submissions (documented as intentional). Consider adding a note or basic email validation regex — currently 'a' would pass as email.

useLenis / Header: nav smooth scroll works but console prints framer-motion warning: 'Please ensure that the container has a non-static position, like relative, fixed, or absolute to ensure scroll offset is calculated correctly.' Consider adding position:relative to the scroll container / motion elements to silence and ensure correct parallax offsets.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Only real issue is Stats.jsx first counter stuck at 0+. Hero 'headline missing' in test log was a false negative — the headline text 'Protecting your cloud, investing in your future' IS rendered (split across masked line reveal spans), confirmed in screenshot. All nav, mobile menu, services accordion (0/1/2), partners marquee, contact form validation, success toast, and form clear behaviours work correctly. Framer-motion whileInView reveals do require gradual scroll; use element.scrollIntoView + 2-3s wait.
rca of the issue: Stats first counter: The Counter useEffect schedules a requestAnimationFrame and returns a cleanup that calls cancelAnimationFrame(raf). Because 'raf' is captured by closure but reassigned inside tick(), under React 18 StrictMode dev double-invoke the cleanup from the first invocation may cancel the RAF just scheduled by the second invocation, leaving n=0 permanently. Only affects i=0 because delay/timing lines up so subsequent counters escape the race. Fix: move raf into a useRef and add a final setN(value) fallback when inView flips true; also guard the effect with an isMountedRef.
